name: Timed-Advance / Autoplay Intent
summary: Unattended auto-advance through an ordered sequence — per-item timing, pause-on-interaction, resume, loop, and a Screen Wake Lock so the display does not sleep during kiosk/autoplay. Extends the carousel block's autoplay, which is single-rate: this generalises it to per-item durations and adds the Wake Lock layer, as the timed member of the advanceable-sequence family (#1179). The boundary behavior (loop / stop / rewind at the end) defers to advanceable-sequence; this intent owns the timing, the pause model, and the wake-lock. Composes the Motion intent (reduced-motion still applies to the transitions it triggers).

timing.description: How long each item is shown. uniform = a single interval for every item (the carousel baseline); per-item = a per-slide/per-item duration (a title slide lingers, a dense slide longer), declared on the item.

pause.description: Whether user interaction suspends auto-advance. on-interaction = hovering/focusing/touching pauses, resuming after (the APG-conformant default); manual-only = autoplay runs until an explicit pause control (kiosk/signage).

wakeLock.description: Whether a Screen Wake Lock is held while autoplay runs. held = request the Wake Lock so the screen stays on during a kiosk/presentation (released on pause/exit); none = let the display sleep per system policy.

description:
Hands-off advance

A deck on a kiosk, a signage carousel, an auto-playing walkthrough: the sequence advances itself on a timer. The Timed-Advance intent owns the timing, the pause behavior, and keeping the screen awake.

Generalises the carousel kernel

The carousel block already ships autoplay, but at a single rate with pause-on-hover/focus. This intent lifts that to per-item durations and adds Screen Wake Lock, as the timed member of the advanceable-sequence family (#1179). End-of-sequence behavior (loop / rewind / stop) is the family's boundary axis, not re-declared here.

Accessibility

  • Pause control required: auto-advancing content must offer a pause (WCAG 2.2.2); pause: on-interaction is the conformant default.
  • Reduced motion: the transitions auto-advance triggers still honor prefers-reduced-motion via the Motion intent.
